title: Payload Audit Drift Classification
goal: Implement #446 so source payload audit output classifies drift instead of reporting raw mismatches.
non_goals: Do not broaden into payload slimming or feature-tier work from later lanes.

intent.outcome: Payload audit output distinguishes transient/generated/intentional-source-extra/missing-root/unexpected-root/expected-projection drift so proof answers the actual trust question.
intent.constraints: Keep scope bounded to package payload audit behavior and tests.
intent.latitude: Choose the smallest classification shape that keeps false positives out of failure pressure.
intent.escalation: Escalate if the audit needs a broader package payload redesign.

execution.next_step: Inspect current payload/source install-sync checks and identify where raw mismatches are produced.
execution.proof: focused planning payload audit tests plus proof selector

invariants: Classify drift without turning transient/generated artifacts into failing payload drift.
Keep package boundaries explicit and avoid adding first-contact surfaces.

intent_continuity.larger intended outcome: #442: make the plan -> implement -> evaluate continuation loop first-class for long lanes.

required_continuation.required follow-on detail: #447-#448 remain open after this slice, and #442 remains open until the loop itself is first-class.
required_continuation.activation trigger: after #446 closeout, promote the next issue in the dogfooding lane

parent_lane.title: Add an autopilot intent loop for plan, implement, evaluate, and continue

iterative_follow_through.next likely slice: continue the current milestone until the completion criteria are met

delegated_judgment.requested outcome: Classify source payload audit drift instead of reporting raw mismatches.
delegated_judgment.hard constraints: Do not make bytecode, generated, or intentional source-only files fail the proof lane.
delegated_judgment.agent may decide locally: The exact classification field names and output shape.
delegated_judgment.escalate when: The implementation requires changing install ownership semantics or the package payload model.

context_budget.live working set: state, this execplan, #446 issue, package payload audit/check code and tests
context_budget.tiny resumability note: Next active issue is #446 payload audit drift classification.
context_budget.context-shift triggers: Before changing payload ownership semantics or broad install behavior.

system_intent_alignment.relevant system intent: Proof should answer the actual trust question for the bounded slice.
system_intent_alignment.slice shaping bias: Classify drift narrowly so checks stay useful and cheap.
system_intent_alignment.broader-lane validation question: Did this slice reduce false-positive proof friction without expanding default payload surface?

active_milestone.scope: Keep this execution thread bounded to the promoted TODO item.

immediate_next_action: Find where raw mismatches become warnings or failures.
Add focused tests for transient, intentional source extra, and unexpected drift.
invariants: Preserve the planning contract and keep the work bounded to this plan.
validation_commands: cd packages/planning && uv run pytest tests/test_installer.py -q -k "payload or drift or source"

uv run agentic-workspace proof --target . --changed .agentic-workspace/planning/state.toml .agentic-workspace/planning/execplans/payload-audit-drift-classification.plan.json packages/planning/src/repo_planning_bootstrap/installer.py packages/planning/tests/test_installer.py --format json
uv run agentic-workspace doctor --target . --modules planning --format json
completion_criteria: Payload audit output classifies drift instead of returning only raw mismatches.
Bytecode and generated/transient artifacts are ignored or classified without failing the lane.
Intentional package-source extras can be declared and preserved.
Tests cover at least one transient file, one intentional source extra, and one real unexpected drift case.

closure_check.evidence carried forward: #445 external intent refresh adapter landed and #446 is now active.
closure_check.reopen trigger: payload audit still reports raw mismatches or false-positive drift
